description: The device integrates a TMS320C64x+ fixed-point VLIW core capable of issuing up to eight operations per cycle. It includes dedicated pipeline resources optimized for DSP workloads like MAC, bit manipulation, and address generation. The core operates with a programmable interrupt model and supports low-latency context switching. It interfaces tightly with on-chip memories and the EDMA3 for high-throughput data movement. Cache/SRAM partitioning options enable tailoring for deterministic or cached operation.
evidence: SPRU732 (C64x+/CPU and Instruction Set Reference Guide)
dependencies: PLL/clock configured; Interrupts initialized (ISTP, IER/IFR); Memory map valid
Remarks: Use CSR.GIE gating and INTMUX routing carefully to maintain deterministic latency in real-time paths.

description: Separate L1 instruction (L1P) and data (L1D) memories can be configured as cache or SRAM. Programmable partitioning allows deterministic scratchpad or caching strategies per application. Proper configuration reduces external memory bandwidth pressure and improves performance. Coherency with EDMA requires cache maintenance or cache-bypass regions as appropriate. The design enables fine-grained tuning for throughput versus determinism.
dependencies: PLL stable; Cache controller enabled; MAR/Cache settings applied before EDMA
Remarks: Define memory attributes and apply cache maintenance around EDMA transfers to avoid stale or dirty lines.

description: The on-chip L2 memory provides a unified SRAM/Cache resource shared by code and data. Its size and mode are device-specific as documented in the data manual. L2 can be used as fast SRAM for real-time buffers or as cache to accelerate external DDR accesses. Configuring L2 complements L1 behavior to achieve target latency and hit rates. Careful placement of stacks and hot data in L2 improves determinism.
dependencies: PLL stable; L2 configuration set early in startup
Remarks: Partition L2 to reserve critical buffers as SRAM when meeting hard real-time deadlines.

description: A programmable PLL with divider network generates system and peripheral clocks. Standard procedures include entering bypass, programming PLLM and PLLDIVs, aligning dividers, issuing GO, and polling for lock. Correct sequencing ensures stable operation across voltage and temperature. Post-dividers allow deriving appropriate clocks for subsystems. Exiting bypass only after lock avoids glitching downstream modules.
evidence: Data Manual (PLLCTL, PLLM, PLLDIVx, PLLCMD, PLLSTAT)
dependencies: Power-on reset complete; Bypass mode entry; Stable input reference clock
Remarks: Follow TI-recommended lock and alignment sequence; avoid register writes without verifying PLLSTAT.

description: The PSC manages power domains and clock gating for modules. Software programs MDCTLx.NEXT and issues PTCMD.GO, then polls PTSTAT and verifies MDSTATx to transition modules to ENABLE. Each peripheral must be enabled in PSC before accessing its registers. Proper sequencing reduces inrush and ensures clock availability. Disabling unused modules saves power without impacting active domains.
evidence: Data Manual (PTCMD, PTSTAT, MDCTLx, MDSTATx, PDCTLx)
dependencies: PLL/clock stable; Domain clocks present; Dependencies resolved per power domain
Remarks: Gate clocks for unused peripherals; sequence domain transitions to meet inter-module dependencies.

description: Hardware events are routed to CPU maskable interrupts INT4–INT15 via INTMUX1–INTMUX3. The core supports IER for masking and IFR for status/acknowledge, with the vector table base set by ISTP. Proper mapping and clearing of flags are required to avoid spurious service. Global enable is controlled by CSR.GIE after all vectors and masks are configured. This mechanism underpins low-latency real-time interrupt handling.
dependencies: PLL stable; Vector table in memory; Events mapped; Pending flags cleared
Remarks: Reserve high-priority vectors for time-critical ISRs and keep handlers short to minimize jitter.

description: The EDMA3 subsystem offloads bulk and scatter-gather transfers between memories and peripherals. It comprises a Channel Controller (TPCC) and one or more Transfer Controllers (TPTC). Software programs PaRAM sets (OPT, SRC/DST, counts, indexes) and enables events to trigger transfers. Completion is detected via IPR/IPRH or serviced by interrupts. Queue configuration and channel mapping enable QoS and bandwidth partitioning.
evidence: EDMA3 (TPCC/TPTC) — DCHMAP, DMAQNUM, OPT, PaRAM, ER/EER, IPR/IPRH
dependencies: PSC enable for EDMA CC/TC; Interrupts/Events configured; Memory/cache attributes set
Remarks: Use linking/chaining for continuous streams; clear EMR/IPR before enabling to avoid stale events.

description: GPIO provides input/output control with atomic set/clear and edge-triggered interrupts. Direction is set per pin, and bank-level enables aggregate interrupts. Rising and falling edge controls are provided via dedicated set/clear registers. Status and acknowledgment are managed in INTSTAT and at the CPU level. EDMA event outputs may be available for timestamping or buffering use cases.
dependencies: PSC enable for GPIO; PINMUX configured; INTMUX routing if interrupts used
Remarks: Prefer SET_DATA/CLR_DATA for atomic output updates; clear pending edges before enabling BINTEN.

description: The I2C module supports master and slave modes with programmable clocking. Key registers include PSC, SCLL/H, SA, CNT, CON, STAT, IE, and DATA for transfers. Typical master transactions program addressing and counts, then drive START/STOP via CON while servicing STAT. Interrupt-driven or polled operation is supported. FIFO control and extended data features improve throughput and flexibility.
evidence: SPRUF94 (I2C_PSC, I2C_SCLL/H, I2C_SA, I2C_CNT, I2C_CON, I2C_STAT, I2C_IE, I2C_DATA)
dependencies: PSC enable for I2C; PINMUX configured; Pull-ups present; INTMUX if interrupts used
Remarks: Compute SCL timings carefully for target bitrate and acknowledge/clear STAT bits per transfer phase.

description: The UART implements a 16550-style register set with baud divisors and FIFOs. Registers include RBR/THR, IER, IIR/FCR, LCR, MCR, LSR, MSR, SCR, and DLL/DLH when DLAB is set. A power/emulation register gates module clocks for low power. Typical init sets line format, divisors, and FIFO control. Interrupt-driven TX/RX improves efficiency over polled I/O.
evidence: SPRUF93 (RBR/THR, IER, IIR/FCR, LCR, DLL/DLH, PWREMU_MGMT)
dependencies: PSC enable for UART; PINMUX configured; INTMUX if interrupts used
Remarks: Write PWREMU_MGMT to release from reset before accessing data path; verify LSR before TX/RX.

description: The SPI module supports master and slave operation with configurable frame formats. Control spans global reset/enable, pin control, per-format timing, and chip-select default polarity. Data is written to SPIDAT and read from SPIBUF with status flags. Interrupt levels and flags are available for event-driven I/O. Proper polarity/phase selection ensures interoperability with connected devices.
evidence: SPRUF92 (SPIGCR0/1, SPIPCx, SPIFMTn, SPIDATn, SPIBUF, SPILVL, SPIFLG)
dependencies: PSC enable for SPI; PINMUX configured; INTMUX if interrupts used
Remarks: Use separate SPIFMT entries for devices with differing modes; clear SPIBUF flags before enabling interrupts.

description: McASP provides flexible time-division multiplexed audio I/O with independent transmit and receive clocks. It offers serializer configuration, frame sync generation, and extensive formatting control. A global state machine coordinates activation across sub-blocks. Data movement typically uses EDMA for sustained throughput. Proper pin function and direction settings are required before enabling streams.
dependencies: PSC enable for McASP; PINMUX configured; EDMA channels set; Clock sources stable
Remarks: Follow the documented GBLCTL bring-up sequence and verify frame syncs before enabling serializers.

feature_name: 3-Port Ethernet Switch with SGMII and MDIO
description: The integrated 3-port Ethernet subsystem includes switch, SGMII, and MDIO management. Control spans port configuration, ALE table, statistics, and link management. MDIO registers allow PHY discovery and configuration. SGMII status must be stable before enabling traffic. Packet movement uses the subsystem-defined DMA path coordinated with EDMA policies.
dependencies: PSC enable for 3PSW; SGMII clock/link; MDIO clock configured; Pinmux set
Remarks: Initialize ALE and default VLAN/ingress rules and verify link via MDIO before forwarding.

description: The DDR controller provides access to external SDRAM with programmable timing. Initialization requires VTP/PHY calibration, timing registers programming, refresh enable, and mode register setup. Stable operation depends on precise timing relative to the input clock. Once configured, it enables high-bandwidth, low-latency external memory access. Thorough memory tests are recommended after bring-up.
dependencies: PSC enable for DDR; PLL clocks stable; VTP/PHY calibration complete
Remarks: Follow the exact initialization sequence from SPRUF85 and validate with stress tests before production use.

description: The PCI module supports master and slave transactions depending on system role. It exposes configuration space and local bridge registers on the DSP side. High-throughput data exchange typically leverages EDMA with careful endian and byte-lane mapping. BAR windows and address translation must be configured correctly. Interrupts and error reporting follow standard PCI semantics.
evidence: SPRUF86 (PCI user’s guide: local regs, config space)
dependencies: PSC enable for PCI; Reference clock present; EDMA configured for data paths
Remarks: Validate endian and BAR mappings in early bring-up; test both master and target cycles.

description: UHPI allows an external host to access DSP memory and registers directly. Control registers manage addressing, data width, and ownership, with interrupt signaling to coordinate transfers. It can accelerate control/data exchange without PCI. Reset and emulation interactions require careful handling. Proper mode selection ensures compatibility with the chosen host.
dependencies: PSC enable for UHPI; Host interface clock; Interrupt routing if used
Remarks: Define ownership and reset strategy to prevent bus contention; document host access windows.

description: Timers provide periodic and one-shot timing with optional split 32-bit mode. Programming includes global control, counter, and period registers. They can generate interrupts for scheduling and capture tasks. Proper clock enable and mapping via INTMUX are required. Watchdog-like use cases are possible depending on device configuration.
dependencies: PSC enable for Timers; INTMUX mapping; Clock source stable
Remarks: Use separate timer instances for timebase and jitter-sensitive tasks to avoid contention.

description: Device configuration registers select pin functions and reflect boot status. PINMUX registers route signals between peripheral functions and GPIO. Correct configuration is prerequisite to peripheral access. Some pads support pull and receiver control. Misconfiguration can tri-state critical signals.
dependencies: PSC enabled as needed; Apply PINMUX before peripheral init; Consult ball map
Remarks: Centralize pinmux settings and audit against schematic to prevent functional pin conflicts.

description: Boot modes and strap pins select initial loading paths like SPI, I2C, NAND/NOR, PCI, or UHPI. Reset cause/status registers indicate the source of reset for diagnostics. Endianness selection may be latched at boot time. Understanding boot flow aids in secondary loader and debug design. Correct strap settings are critical for reliable startup.
dependencies: Power/clock valid; Strap pins; External boot memory or host path available
Remarks: Capture boot pins in early software logs and provide a recovery path for mis-strapped systems.

description: An IEEE 1149.1 JTAG interface provides emulation, breakpoint, and trace support. Emulation may influence clock or power gating behavior for safety. Some peripherals require clocks running during debug access. Debug features enable intrusive and non-intrusive analysis. Proper isolation from production timing is advised.
dependencies: JTAG connection; Clocks running for accessed modules
Remarks: Document emulation side effects and disable low-power gating when attaching a debugger.

description: Peripherals can raise EDMA sync events to automate data movement without CPU polling. Channels are mapped to queues to balance bandwidth and latency. Completion and error conditions are reported via IPR/IER and EMR. This enables sustained streaming for audio, Ethernet, and storage interfaces. Proper clearing of flags avoids missed events.
dependencies: PSC enable for EDMA; Peripheral sync events; INTMUX mapping for completion IRQs
Remarks: Pre-clear pending events and use chaining for continuous producer/consumer pipelines.

description: GPIO provides dedicated registers for atomic set and clear of outputs. Using these avoids read-modify-write hazards in concurrent contexts. This is effective for ISR-safe signaling. The feature complements OUTDATA access for readback. Combined with bank interrupts, it enables deterministic control loops.
dependencies: PSC enable for GPIO; Proper pin direction; Pinmux set to GPIO
Remarks: Prefer atomic registers in ISRs to avoid race conditions with background tasks.

description: The MDIO block manages external PHY discovery, link status, and configuration. Software accesses user channels to read/write PHY registers. Live and link bitmaps provide quick status of connected PHYs. Correct MDC clock programming ensures timing compliance. MDIO is foundational to establishing SGMII/RGMII links.
dependencies: PSC enable for 3PSW/MDIO; MDIO clock configured; External PHY present
Remarks: Poll link state before enabling MAC/switch traffic and handle PHY-specific quirks in a table.
